whyNotes.nom_sg: Hrad-type nouns have a zero ending (bare stem) in nominative singular.
whyNotes.gen_sg: Hrad-type nouns take -u in genitive singular (e.g. hradu).
whyNotes.dat_sg: Hrad-type nouns take -u in dative singular (e.g. hradu).
whyNotes.acc_sg: Hrad-type nouns take the same form as nominative in accusative. For inanimate nouns, accusative always looks the same as nominative.
whyNotes.voc_sg: Hrad-type nouns take -e in vocative singular (e.g. hrade). Watch out: some consonants change before -e: h→z, k→c, r→ř, ch→š.
whyNotes.loc_sg: Hrad-type nouns take -ě or -u in locative singular (e.g. na hradě). Watch out: some consonants change before -ě: k→c, h→z, r→ř, d→ď, t→ť, n→ň.
whyNotes.ins_sg: Hrad-type nouns take -em in instrumental singular (e.g. hradem).
whyNotes.nom_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -y in nominative plural (e.g. hrady).
whyNotes.gen_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -ů in genitive plural (e.g. hradů).
whyNotes.dat_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -ům in dative plural (e.g. hradům).
whyNotes.acc_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -y in accusative plural — same as nominative plural. For inanimate nouns, accusative always looks the same as nominative.
whyNotes.voc_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -y in vocative plural. Vocative plural is always the same as nominative plural.
whyNotes.loc_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -ech in locative plural (e.g. hradech).
whyNotes.ins_pl: Hrad-type nouns take -y in instrumental plural (e.g. hrady).

whyNotes.nom_sg: Muž-type nouns have a zero ending (bare stem) in nominative singular.

whyNotes.dat_sg: Žena-type nouns take -ě in dative singular (e.g. ženě). Watch out: some consonants change before -ě: k→c, h→z, r→ř.
whyNotes.acc_sg: Žena-type nouns take -u in accusative singular (e.g. ženu).

whyNotes.nom_sg: Kuře-type nouns end in -e in nominative singular (e.g. kuře).

whyNotes.dat_sg: Kuře-type nouns take -eti in dative singular (e.g. kuřeti). The -et- stem extension appears in all singular cases except nominative, accusative, and vocative.
whyNotes.acc_sg: Kuře-type nouns keep the same form as nominative in accusative (e.g. kuře). Neuter nouns always have accusative = nominative.

whyNotes.nom_pl: Kuře-type nouns take -ata in nominative plural (e.g. kuřata). The plural stem changes to -at-.
whyNotes.gen_pl: Kuře-type nouns take -at in genitive plural (e.g. kuřat).

whyNotes.ins_pl: Kuře-type nouns take -aty in instrumental plural (e.g. kuřaty).
